ops.title: getaway v2: the journey is the unit
ops.body:
# getaway v2 rulings: the journey is the unit

Supersedes "Hybrid award routing: direct vs gateway + cash positioning" (03b201f). The two rulings that die outright: "Returns are a second Workflow invocation" (its premise — return inputs don't exist until the outbound is picked — stopped holding once the shortlist yields return endpoints mid-run and `/search` takes comma-delimited lists both sides) and the 240-minute cash-cabin cutoff (baked judgment; deleted from code and doctrine).

## The journey is the unit of search, ranking, and presentation

One normalized Journey representation for direct, hybrid, round-trip, and open-jaw results: one concrete outbound (plus onward/bridge legs on hybrids, typed `award|cash`) paired with one concrete return leg on round trips. Pairing happens before ranking; finalize only formats. Returns sweep in-run — endpoints resolve lazily from the outbound shortlist ∪ declared `onward_dests`, one comma-listed `/search` call. Hybrids compose at expand into the same journeys list; assess judges whole journeys, cash hops included. Composition, fit facts, cost vectors, and preference misses are deterministic CLI code — never agent prompts.

## Search states are honest, and leads stay visible

Per-endpoint, per-leg search states: `complete | searched_empty | partial{reason, has_more} | not_run{reason} | failed{reason, retryability}`. Unpaired outbounds (return `searched_empty`) are a visible trailing lead class ordered by outbound mileage with cache age — an expired cached empty reads "unverified". `partial`/`not_run`/`failed` never masquerade as "no space".

## Cost competition happens in the rank cost lane

Composition never prunes by cost. Every shape competes as a journey: same-program journeys band on scalar bookable mileage; mixed-program and cash-bearing journeys rank as per-program vectors (cash cents its own axis) on a Pareto front. No cent-per-point constant exists anywhere.

## Cabin is decided per leg, by judgment

The cash-or-award cabin call happens leg by leg, decided by model judgment fed by duration fit facts — no fixed cutoff constant, no `cash_cutoff_minutes` annotation anywhere.

## Lodging derives from observed clocks

Stay intervals come from actual journey timestamps — a cash onward leg's arrival comes from the priced fli quote's real arrival datetime; an unknown arrival defers lodging (`deferred{unknown_arrival}`), never guesses a night. Lodging scope is explicit in the plan; stays spend zero seats.aero quota.

## Surviving rulings, carried forward

- **Vetoes bind endpoints only**: the avoid union vetoes where a trip may end, never where it passes through; `trip set` rejects an `onward_dests` set intersecting the union.
- **Hybrid fan-out is bounded**: only the plan's declared `gateways`/`onward_dests`, capped by `hybrid.max_hybrids`, cheap-ranked before any detail expansion spends quota.
- **Hybrid-absent is inert — structurally**: a plan without a `hybrid` key compiles zero hybrid nodes.
- **Quota at the boundary** (upgraded): every `SeatsClient._get()` call runs reserve/release/reconcile under the store flock — reservation before HTTP, lock released during, monotonic header reconcile after; parallel processes can't jointly cross the `--quota-floor`. The old "trust each agent's reported quota_remaining" discipline is obsolete.
ops.when: Before modifying journey composition, return handling, hybrid routing, lodging intervals, or the finalists board in getaway.
